_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket index, an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices benchmarked to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase since 2015. This rise reflects a recent and uneven price climb rather than a steady trend. The index captures global commodity prices which are subject to periodic shocks. It provides a consolidated view of everyday soft commodities important to many consumers.
notes.movers: Within the basket, coffee prices have surged by 93%, the largest increase, followed by orange juice at 22%. Wheat and sugar have shown more modest gains of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price changes largely stem from specific weather events and crop diseases—such as frost and drought in arabica coffee regions and citrus greening along with hurricane damage affecting orange juice production. Soft commodities are traded in relatively thin markets, where such shocks can cause pronounced price volatility absent broad inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven primarily by a few crops vulnerable to weather and biological risks. Because these commodities have limited supply flexibility, their prices can remain elevated over multi-year periods after adverse events. Market participants should view these movements as concentrated disruptions rather than systemic inflation trends.
